stepMetrics.note: first registry write used the wrong shape for namedEntities

issues.description: build_s05() used Pt(0.7) for the title zone x-coordinate instead of Inches(0.7), placing the title off the visible slide area
issues.fix: Changed Pt(0.7) to Inches(0.7) in build_s05() title zone positioning
issues.preventionMethod: Add runtime assertion in add_textbox() that rejects Pt values > 72 for positional arguments

issues.description: Three consecutive body slides (s04–s06) all used symmetric two-column layouts, creating visual monotony despite different content forms
issues.fix: Redesigned s05 to use an asymmetric 60/40 split with a side-stripe decoration to break the pattern
issues.preventionMethod: s05_validate_plan.py already checks layout variety quota — threshold may need tightening for runs of identical patterns

issues.description: Card background rounded rectangles in build_s07() used RGBColor('CADCFC') without the 0x prefix, causing a ValueError at runtime
issues.fix: Changed to RGBColor(0xCA, 0xDC, 0xFC) using the three-integer form
issues.preventionMethod: Include a RGBColor usage reminder in build-script-template.md and add a hex-string lint check

issues.description: Hero stat number on s03 used Inches(44) for font size instead of Pt(44), resulting in an enormous invisible text frame
issues.fix: Changed Inches(44) to Pt(44) in the stat display font size assignment
issues.preventionMethod: Same unit validation helper as issue-01

issues.description: LibreOffice headless render produced slightly different gradient fills compared to PowerPoint, making the QA subagent flag false-positive color contrast issues on s01 cover
issues.fix: Not fixed — documented as known limitation. Gradient rendering is a known LibreOffice fidelity gap

recommendations.title: Add unit validation to s07_slide_helpers.py positional arguments
recommendations.description: Three of five issues were Inches/Pt/Emu confusion in s07-build.py. Adding runtime assertions to add_textbox() and add_shape() that reject Pt values > 72 for x/y/w/h positional parameters, and Inches values > 2 for font size parameters, would catch these at generation time instead of visual QA.

estimatedPayoff.affectedSessions: every session that writes a s07-build.py from scratch

recommendations.title: Add consecutive-layout-pattern check to s05_validate_plan.py
recommendations.description: The existing layout variety quota checks global distribution but not local runs. Adding a sliding-window check (no more than 2 consecutive body slides with the same layout family) would catch visual monotony earlier, before s07-build.py is written.

recommendations.title: Document LibreOffice gradient rendering gap in QA reviewer prompt
recommendations.description: The QA subagent flagged a false positive on gradient color contrast because LibreOffice renders gradients differently from PowerPoint. Adding a note in qa-reviewer-prompt.md to treat gradient-related contrast issues as MINOR when rendered via LibreOffice would reduce false positives.

estimatedPayoff.affectedSessions: any session rendered via LibreOffice with gradient backgrounds

workedWell: VLM image selection in Step 5f worked well — the selected cover image scored highest on all 5 criteria and no fallbacks were triggered. The 3-variation strategy justified its cost.
The bottom-up slide-count derivation in Step 3c landed on 10 — exactly matching the user's later expressed expectation, validating that we did not need to fight the act structure to hit a target.
worthSurprise: 3 of 5 issues (60%) were unit confusion in s07-build.py (Inches vs Pt vs raw integers). This is the single highest-leverage area for code quality improvement — a type-safe unit wrapper or runtime guard would eliminate the entire category.
wouldChange: The design self-review in Step 7a caught 0 of the 3 code-quality issues because it operates at the design-intent level, not the code level. Consider adding a lightweight code-lint pass between Step 7 (write s07-build.py) and Step 8a (execute it).
